description:
You are a technical writer who has just inherited an unfinished documentation project that lives in  

    /home/user/doc-project  

Current directory structure (already present before you start):

    /home/user/doc-project/
    ├── markdown/
    │   ├── intro.md
    │   └── setup.md
    └── (nothing else)

Existing Markdown files (already written for you):

1. /home/user/doc-project/markdown/intro.md  
   ```
   # Introduction

   This is the introduction document.
   ```

2. /home/user/doc-project/markdown/setup.md  
   ```
   # Setup

   Follow these steps to set up the project.
   ```

Task  
Create an automation-oriented Makefile in /home/user/doc-project that lets any colleague perform common documentation actions with a single command. The Makefile must define exactly the six phony targets listed below (no more, no fewer). All targets must work when the user runs make from inside /home/user/doc-project.  After you finish, the automated grading system will run make stats and make clean to be sure everything works.

Required phony targets (names are case-sensitive):

1. all          – the default target; it must depend on build.  
2. build        – (a) create an output directory /home/user/doc-project/html if it does not exist,  
                  (b) for every *.md file in /home/user/doc-project/markdown produce a corresponding
                      *.html file inside html/ **with the exact same base name**.  
                  HTML conversion rule: wrap the full textual contents of the source file inside
                  a <pre>…</pre> block and prepend a first line comment indicating the source,
                  exactly in this form (angle brackets included):  
                      \<!-- generated from intro.md --\>  
                  The automated test will open html/intro.html and html/setup.html to verify:
                      * First line is the comment shown above (with correct file name).  
                      * Second line is “<pre>” and the last line is “</pre>”.  
3. stats        – write a log file /home/user/doc-project/build/docs_stats.log that contains **only
                  two lines** (plus a trailing newline):  
                      Files: <N>  
                      Lines: <M>  
                  where <N> is the number of *.md files and <M> is the total line count across all
                  markdown files (blank lines included).  Create the directory build/ if necessary.
4. zip          – create /home/user/doc-project/documentation.zip that contains the entire html/
                  directory (and nothing else) preserving paths.
5. serve        – start a local web server rooted at html/ on port 8000 using
                  “python3 -m http.server 8000” (do **not** daemonize; this target is for
                  interactive use only, so it should run until interrupted).
6. clean        – remove the directories html/ and build/ and the file documentation.zip if they
                  exist, but leave everything under markdown/ untouched.

Makefile style requirements:

• A single “.PHONY” line must list **all six** target names exactly:  
  .PHONY: all build stats zip serve clean

• Each command recipe line must begin with a tab character (standard GNU make behavior).  
• Do not use shell utilities that are unlikely to exist in a minimal container (stick to coreutils,
  awk, sed, grep, find, zip, python3, etc.).  
• The file must be named exactly “Makefile” (capital M, no extension) and live directly in
  /home/user/doc-project.

Verification notes the grader will follow:

1. It will run “make -C /home/user/doc-project” (implicit default target) – this must generate
   html/intro.html and html/setup.html.  
2. It will run “make -C /home/user/doc-project stats” – this must create build/docs_stats.log whose
   content matches the two-line format precisely (no extra spaces or blank lines).  
3. It will open the Makefile to confirm that the only phony targets are the six requested, listed
   in a single .PHONY line.  
4. Finally it will run “make -C /home/user/doc-project clean” and expect html/, build/ and
   documentation.zip to be gone.

Do not display any explanatory prose once you begin writing commands; only command output is fine.  
Your deliverable is the correctly authored /home/user/doc-project/Makefile (and any directories or
files the Makefile itself generates once invoked).  No other files should be modified.
truth:
Directory tree and file state *before* the agent starts: /home/user/doc-project/ ├── markdown/ │ ├── intro.md (4 lines exactly, ending with a newline) │ │ 1 "# Introduction" │ │ 2 "" │ │ 3 "This is the introduction document." │ │ 4 "" │ └── setup.md (4 lines exactly, ending with a newline) │ 1 "# Setup" │ 2 "" │ 3 "Follow these steps to set up the project." │ 4 "" └── (Makefile does not exist yet) Ground-truth expectations *after* the tests run (i.e., after “make”, “make stats” and before “make clean”): 1. /home/user/doc-project/Makefile exists. • Contains a single line beginning “.PHONY: all build stats zip serve clean”. • Contains six corresponding targets all:, build:, stats:, zip:, serve:, clean: (order not important). • No additional target names appear in the .PHONY declaration. 2. /home/user/doc-project/html/ directory exists with: a) intro.html whose first three lines are: 1 “” 2 “
”  
      3 “# Introduction”  
      … (last line of file is “
”). b) setup.html with analogous pattern, first line exactly “”. 3. /home/user/doc-project/build/docs_stats.log exists with exactly (including final newline): Files: 2 Lines: 8 4. /home/user/doc-project/documentation.zip exists and, when unzipped, yields a single top-level directory html/ containing intro.html and setup.html with the same relative paths. 5. After “make clean” the paths /home/user/doc-project/html, /home/user/doc-project/build, /home/user/doc-project/documentation.zip must all be absent, while markdown/ and its contents remain untouched.
